name: Palolo
desc: Deep valley residential pocket mauka of Kaimuki — quiet and green deeper in, but forum warnings about rougher pockets.
insider: The deeper you go into the valley the safer and greener it gets — the back of Palolo is genuinely lush and quiet
Palolo Valley stream trail is a true hidden local hike — zero tourists, just neighborhood dog walkers
Community garden plots at the Palolo Valley Community Garden are available and affordable — great for growing tropical produce
Street parking is tight — most homes have single-car carports at best, and guests park on narrow valley roads
The mouth of the valley near Palolo Ave has rougher pockets — car break-ins and occasional property crime are reported
Palolo Homes (public housing) at the valley entrance has had crime issues — the valley improves dramatically past it
The valley dead-ends at the Koolau ridge — there's only one road in and out, which keeps through traffic nonexistent
Homes are significantly cheaper than neighboring Kaimuki — you can find houses under $900K that would be $1.3M one ridge over
The Palolo Valley District Park has a nice pool and basketball courts — a genuine community gathering spot
Wild pigs and mongoose are common in the upper valley — secure trash cans and don't leave pet food outside
